message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-2hrfs (find_cycle undirected direction)

Fixed in commit 6d541dd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-19x8x","title":"parity: ISMAGS class missing from isomorphism API","description":"NetworkX exposes networkx.algorithms.isomorphism.ISMAGS(graph, subgraph, node_match=None, edge_match=None, cache=None) with is_isomorphic(), isomorphisms_iter(), subgraph_is_isomorphic(), subgraph_isomorphisms_iter(), and largest_common_subgraph(). FrankenNetworkX does not export ISMAGS. Add an ISMAGS wrapper that converts FrankenNetworkX graph objects via _networkx_graph_for_parity and delegates to NetworkX, plus parity tests for constructor signature and core methods.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:25:45.112099160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:30:32.434165705Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T09:30:32.432309961Z","close_reason":"Completed in 2c0f4aa","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-1ewpw","title":"parity: transitive_closure_dag silent on undirected; weakly_connected_components / topological_generations have wrong error wording","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-yf4tf and br-r37-c1-4elbw. Four more drifts: (1) transitive_closure_dag silently runs on undirected input; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for undirected type'). (2) topological_generations on undirected raises NetworkXError('Topological generations not defined on undirected graphs.'); nx raises NetworkXError('Topological sort not defined on undirected graphs.'). (3) weakly_connected_components on undirected raises 'weakly_connected_components is not defined for undirected graphs. Use connected_components instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (4) number_weakly_connected_components has the same divergence. Fix: align all four to nx's exact error wording.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:34:50.428220161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:37:55.592999258Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:37:55.590754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 8c1b91c. weakly_connected_components, number_weakly_connected_components, topological_generations, and transitive_closure_dag now raise with nx's exact error wording. 8 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-24wk3","title":"parity: biconnected_component_edges returns list, nx returns generator","description":"fnx.biconnected_component_edges(G) returns a list while nx returns a generator. Same iter contract issue as br-r37-c1-682kr (8 traversal/path/clique iterators) and br-r37-c1-ohxpp (edge_boundary). Drop-in code doing isinstance(result, types.GeneratorType) breaks; short-circuit short-circuits like next(biconnected_component_edges(huge_graph)) materialise the entire result on fnx vs. one component on nx. Fix: convert wrapper to generator function.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:07:49.555743747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:09:51.212476827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:09:51.210630672Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 129af9c. biconnected_component_edges now returns a true generator. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-2hrfs","title":"find_cycle (undirected) edge-tuple direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.find_cycle on an undirected graph yields edges in algorithm-canonical orientation, but nx yields edges in DFS-traversal direction (which depends on adj-iteration order).

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'), ('a','b'), ('b','c'), ('d','e'), ('a','c')]
  fnx.find_cycle(g) -> [('c','a'), ('a','b'), ('b','c')]
  nx.find_cycle(gx) -> [('c','b'), ('b','a'), ('a','c')]

Edge tuples are reversed and the cycle is traversed in the opposite DFS direction. Fix: delegate find_cycle to nx for undirected graphs (the directed case already matches).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:04.011873621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:07:02.675590565Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-2hrfs","title":"find_cycle (undirected) edge-tuple direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.find_cycle on an undirected graph yields edges in algorithm-canonical orientation, but nx yields edges in DFS-traversal direction (which depends on adj-iteration order).

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'), ('a','b'), ('b','c'), ('d','e'), ('a','c')]
  fnx.find_cycle(g) -> [('c','a'), ('a','b'), ('b','c')]
  nx.find_cycle(gx) -> [('c','b'), ('b','a'), ('a','c')]

Edge tuples are reversed and the cycle is traversed in the opposite DFS direction. Fix: delegate find_cycle to nx for undirected graphs (the directed case already matches).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:04.011873621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:08:43.076611064Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:08:43.074643528Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-2vdtt","title":"strongly_connected_components order disagrees with nx on multi-component DiGraphs","description":"fnx.strongly_connected_components currently materialises Rust's SCC output and applies reversed() to match nx. The reversed() flip happens to match small-symmetric cases but is wrong on inputs where Rust and nx disagree on component-discovery start node.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a'),('e','f'),('f','e')]
  fnx -> [{'f','e'}, {'a','b','c','d'}]
  nx  -> [{'a','b','c','d'}, {'f','e'}]
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','a'),('c','d'),('d','c')]
  fnx -> [{'c','d'}, {'b','a'}]
  nx  -> [{'b','a'}, {'c','d'}]

Fix: delegate strongly_connected_components to nx for parity (Rust + reversed() heuristic is unsound).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:39.307393335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:02:29.392395805Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:02:29.390417839Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3dxfn","title":"parity: all_pairs_dijkstra_path / all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length outer-key iteration order doesn't match nx","description":"fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path and fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length yield outer-key (source) iteration in arbitrary Rust internal order (e.g. ['a','e','c','d','b']) instead of nx's node-insertion order (['c','d','a','b','e']). Drop-in code that iterates 'for source, dists in result.items(): ...' breaks. Same iteration-order family fixed for triangles/core_number/etc. Note: outer keys for all_pairs_shortest_path[_length] already match nx; only dijkstra variants need the fix.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:35:36.535013167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.955576532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.953597595Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 40d5417. all_pairs_dijkstra_path / _length outer keys now iterate in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4elbw","title":"parity: 4 more functions raise NotImplemented/NetworkXError with custom messages instead of nx's standard wording","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-yf4tf — 4 more functions diverge from nx's exact error messages: (1) condensation on undirected raises 'strongly_connected_components is not defined for undirected graphs. Use connected_components instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) flow_hierarchy on undirected raises NetworkXError('flow_hierarchy requires a DiGraph'); nx raises NetworkXError('G must be a digraph in flow_hierarchy'). (3) in_degree_centrality and (4) out_degree_centrality on undirected raise custom 'is not defined for undirected graphs.' messages; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. Drop-in code matching the exact text breaks. Fix: align all four to nx's exact wording.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:28:50.121233008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.902270771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.900331589Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 35bb5d3. strongly_connected_components/number_strongly/kosaraju/condensation/in_degree_centrality/out_degree_centrality/flow_hierarchy now raise with nx's exact error messages. 17 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
